behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (evaluate command -> artifact store keying), not just pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: behavior is storage routing, not model-quality dependent. MULTI-STEP: single stage write check, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is only about where evaluate writes artifacts ("stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact"), not about model decision quality or multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command against a real in-process artifact repository, then assert `grounding` contains evaluate output and `plan` is unchanged/absent for that write.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears when implement orchestration reads one artifact and writes another through repository interfaces. Unit-level function tests can miss read/write wiring bugs. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: correctness target here is artifact plumbing (read grounding, write plan), not validating model output quality. MULTI-STEP: one stage flow with repository interaction, not full pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is deterministic orchestration ("reads grounding ... writes ... to `plan`") and does not require judging model content.
behaviors.test_description: Seed repository with `grounding`, execute implement flow, assert implement consumed grounding context and persisted execution result to `plan` without mutating `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is conditional side-effect suppression across issue-content parsing + artifact write path. REAL-INFRA: no system primitives needed. LLM-DEP: condition is based on presence of diagnosis content, not model-quality evaluation. MULTI-STEP: single stage side-effect behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow/Agentic because behavior text scopes to deep-dive side effect only ("no longer overwrites `plan`") rather than multi-agent decision chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with artifact repo, and assert no write/update occurs to `plan` while other expected outputs remain intact.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: lineage failure emerges across producer-consumer modules sharing run-context scoping; unit tests of either side alone can pass. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP: requirement is retrieval by lineage/context, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: cross-stage handoff but can be validated in-process without full agentic pipeline execution. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on lineage/readability in same context, not validating full deep-dive->evaluate->implement sequence outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Within one run context, persist `grounding` from evaluate producer path, invoke implement consumer path, and assert correct artifact resolution by run/context identifiers.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Provisional level Workflow. Evidence quote: behavior explicitly requires a "full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`)" and asserts final-stage output reflects earlier-stage grounding. Lower-level miss-proof: Integration tests can validate artifact APIs and pairwise handoffs, but can still miss failures in sequential agent-stage orchestration where earlier grounding is not carried into the final plan during full pipeline execution. REAL-INFRA: not primarily OS/network bound. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: this is a full agent pipeline with sequential stage decisions and handoffs, matching Workflow definition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real end-to-end chain for one run context (deep-dive, evaluate, implement) and assert final `plan` content incorporates grounding facts/constraints created earlier in that same run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: fallback correctness depends on implement orchestration plus artifact-store state transitions (missing grounding, preserve others). REAL-INFRA: no need for real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP: validates fallback and artifact integrity, not generation quality. MULTI-STEP: single implement-stage fallback path. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about safe fallback and artifact corruption boundaries when grounding is absent, which is deterministic storage/orchestration logic.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize run context without `grounding` but with unrelated artifacts, run implement flow, then assert a valid `plan` is created and pre-existing artifacts remain unchanged.
